content.problemStatement:
Finally, College has started calling students back to campus. There are so many students and thus due to some safety measures the college can’t call back all the students on the same day. It currently has the capacity of screening KKK students on a single day. There is a total of NNN students. What's the minimum number of days required for all the students to be back on the campus?


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers N,KN, KN,K.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer - the minimum number of days required for all the students to be back on the campus.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers N and K.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the minimum number of days required, i.e. ceil(N / K).

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: N=3, K=3 → 1 day.\nTest case 2: N=3, K=2 → ceil(3/2)=2 days.\nTest case 3: N=4, K=3 → ceil(4/3)=2 days.
Only one student, capacity is larger, so 1 day.

solution.approach: For each test case we need the smallest integer d such that d*K >= N. This is exactly the ceiling of N/K. Using integer arithmetic the formula d = (N + K - 1) / K works when the addition does not overflow. Because N and K can be as large as 10^21, we must use 128‑bit integers (or arbitrary precision types) for the intermediate sum. The algorithm is O(1) per test case, O(T) overall, and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are stored.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        K = int(next(it))
        days = (N + K - 1) // K
        out.append(str(days))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
